properties.remark: Report of widespread wind damage near the vicinity of Kendall Stone Rd, Pat Dixon Rd, Kirkland Still Rd. Trees down, roof damage, barn damage. Time estimated by radar.

A tornado touched down in the northwestern portions of Clay County, or within the Camp Blanding Wildlife Management Area just to the northwest of Kingsley Lake, on Sunday afternoon around 4:13 PM local time. This tornado was documented by pictures and video as it crossed County Road 225 that were posted on social media. A National Weather team determined that the peak wind was around 90 mph, resulting in snapped and uprooted pine trees along its approximate one mile path. This tornado`s path length may have been longer based on available video evidence, but the survey team was unable to locate any damage beyond the indicated path length in the heavily wooded Camp Blanding Wildlife Management Area. Estimated path width was around 100 yards. The tornado dissipated around 4:18 pm.

A tornado touched down adjacent to the Immanuel Baptist Church off Sarroca Lane, or just southwest of the intersection of Normandy Boulevard and Cecil Commerce Center Parkway, around 5:05 PM. This small tornado, with an estimated maximum width of 25 yards, was captured on video as it flipped a garbage dumpster on the church property. This tornado likely lifted before crossing Normandy Boulevard, with the parent supercell thunderstorm moving northeastward, producing another likely touchdown near the intersection of Brian Lakes Drive and Justin Oaks Drive North, where the tornado was again captured on video as it damaged fences and trees adjacent to homes in the area. This damage represents am estimated peak wind of around 85 mph, and the non-continuous damage path of this tornado was around 1.38 miles. The tornado lifted before reaching Chaffee Trail Elementary School around 5:07.
Public report of Half dollar sized hail near Ratliff FL, in Nassau County.
Quarter sized hail reported to the north of Palm Coast Parkway NE in Palm Coast, FL.
Ping Pong sized hail reported at Bulow Plantation, west of Flagler Beach.

Corrects previous tornado report from 5 NNE Ambrose. Coffee County Emergency management relayed that survey of the damage revealed straight line wind damage, not tornado damage, near the Griffin Rd/Old Coffee Rd/Highway 206 area west of Broxton. Damage consisted of 3 trees down and a report of roof damage. Time estimated by info from EM and radar.
Emer Mgr relayed photo showing a tree down on a mobile home at Davis Landing on the Altamaha River in Appling County, GA. Time est off Radar data.

Coffee County Emergency management relayed multiple reports of a tornado on the ground near the Griffin Rd/Old Coffee Rd/Highway 206 area west of Broxton. Also report of roof damage on Griffin Rd. Time estimated by info from EM and radar.

THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN INACCESSIBLE MARSHY TIMBERLAND IN NORTHEASTERN CLINCH COUNTY SOUTHEAST OF THE COGDELL COMMUNITY, UPROOTING AND SNAPPING SEVERAL TREES AS IT MOVED PARALLEL TO AND JUST SOUTH OF CAMP BRANCH ROAD. THE TORNADO THEN MOVED INTO WARE COUNTY WITH ADDITIONAL TREE DAMAGE NOTED AS IT CROSSED OLD MANOR MILLWOOD ROAD JUST EAST OF THE COUNTY LINE. THE STORM WEAKENED AS IT CONTINUED EAST-NORTHEAST WITH ONLY LIGHT TIMBER DAMAGE WHERE IT CROSSED WINDMILL ROAD. THE TORNADO RE-INTENSIFIED AS IT APPROACHED MANOR MILLWOOD ROAD SOUTH (CR-472) AND PRODUCED THE ONLY STRUCTURAL DAMAGE ALONG ITS PATH. A METAL OUTBUILDING HAD HALF ITS TIN ROOF REMOVED, WITH SOME OF IT TOSSED NEARLY 300 YARDS DOWNWIND.THE ADJACENT HOME SUFFERED SLIGHT ROOF DAMAGE, WHILE SEVERAL TREES ON THE PROPERTY WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED. CONTINUING EAST JUST SOUTH OF HIGHWAY 122, THE TORNADO BEGAN TO WEAKEN A FINAL TIME. IT CROSSED FARMS EDGE ROAD, PRODUCING SPORADIC TREE DAMAGE ALONG AND NEAR THE ROADWAY, BEFORE SHORTLY THEREAFTER. A SPECIAL THANK YOU TO ONE OF OUR GEORGIA MEDIA BROADCAST PARTNERS FOR INFORMATION PERTAINING TO THIS NARRATIVE.
Large tree uprooted, photo on social media. Possible tornado damage. Time estimated from radar.

*** 5 INJ *** A tornado touched down near Santa Fe Road about 2 miles to the southeast of Nahunta around 140 PM and traveled east to northeast snapping trees at the trunk and knocking over a small dog kennel. A large section of a roof was removed to a residence near Matthews Circle. Other higher-end damage in the same area consisted of destruction of a mobile home and debris blown away. This damage represents an estimated peak wind of near 130 mph. The path length was estimated to be about 5 miles. 5 minor injuries occurred with this tornado.
Emergency manager reports significant structural damage to homes on Matthews Circle, possible tornado, possible injuries. Time estimated from radar.

A NWS storm survey discovered damage due to an EF0 tornado with a maximum width of 125 yards that briefly touched down in the Beacon Lake neighborhood in northern St Johns county near US highway 1. The tornado caused damage to roofs, fences, and patio furniture with winds up to 70 to 80 mph at its peak along a path measuring one quarter of a mile.

Visual confirmation of tornado in the World Golf Village in the Trailmark subdivision via media and social media. Excellent radar indication of tornado NWS Survey Team enroute for storm survey to determine path, intensity, length and width of tornado.

*** 1 INJ *** NWS Damage Survey results concluded that began along County Road 13 and extended across portions of the Trailmark and Samara Lakes neighborhoods in western St Johns County. The tornado was estimated at an EF1 intensity with peak winds of 100 mph. The total path length was 6.7 miles and at its widest point was 310 yards. There was one minor injury that occurred as result of the tornado event.
